description_long: This object is a rustic, open-topped bucket or barrel constructed from several dark, irregularly shaped bands that spiral upward to form the cylindrical body. The bands appear to be made of a rough, natural material, possibly wood or a wood-like synthetic, giving the object a hand-crafted, organic look. The structure is hollow, with a wide opening at the top and a sturdy, continuous handle arching from one side to the other, attached at the rim. The handle is thick and integrated into the design, suggesting it is strong enough for carrying moderate loads. The surface of the bands is uneven and matte, with visible texture and slight warping, enhancing the artisanal appearance. There are no visible labels, markings, or reinforcements beyond the layered bands themselves.
description: A rustic, dark, open-topped bucket with a thick, integrated handle and irregular, banded construction. The surface is rough and matte, giving it a hand-crafted, organic appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, thick handle arching upward, and the layered, banded construction of the bucket.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the circular opening, the handle's attachment at the rim, and the spiral banding.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous handle and the uneven, organic texture of the bands.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the handle's vertical rise and the concentric, stacked bands.
